title: radiomics-phantom: synthetic digital phantoms and an IBSI-compliant radiomics core for feature-stability research
description:
An open, fully synthetic research kernel for studying the stability of radiomics features under acquisition variation. It provides deterministic digital texture phantoms with known ground truth, a from-scratch IBSI-compliant feature core, an acquisition simulator, a feature-stability atlas, and physics-based feature normalisation.

No patient data, no DICOM, and no deep learning are involved: every volume is generated from a seed and every feature is computed from first principles using numpy/scipy/scikit-image only.

Code is released under the MIT License; the accompanying prose and figures are released under CC BY 4.0.
